title: Chapter Seventy-One: The Thirty-Six Steps
content:
## JEONG YEON-SHIN

No black ranks. Not one.

Yeon-shin scanned the allied force arrayed before the Blood Flame Sect's main hall and found the absence he had expected. The vast, thrumming energy unique to those who had crossed into the highest realm was nowhere among them. The Central Plains was too large and the Desolate Sect's forces too stretched -- seventeen squads scattered across ten provinces, three committed to the Hwangbo campaign. The main sect had been emptied to its bones.

Blue ranks, though. Plenty of those. They commanded the orthodox martial artists who had been gathered through justification and authority -- men and women who fought because the cause was righteous, or because they had been told it was, which was sometimes the same thing and sometimes not. They clashed fiercely with the Blood Flame Sect's main hall forces, steel on steel, energy waves booming through the courtyard like thunder trapped between walls.

What had come beyond the expected, though, was something else entirely.

"Did you join the Blood Flame Sect!"

A familiar shout, bright and furious. The young Taoist was already running toward him, plum blossom embroidery rippling on his Mount Hua uniform. His lightness technique was accomplished but not elegant -- he ran like a man who wanted to reach his friend, not like one who wanted to look graceful doing it.

*Yu Hyeon.*

The sect leader's disciple. Friend. The same easy grin he had worn the day they first met, even here, even now, amid the stink of blood and burning. A leisure unbefitting the battle situation flowed from him. That was Yu Hyeon's way -- to be exactly himself, regardless of circumstance.

Behind him came Wei Ji Myo-hwa of Zhongnan and Little Divine Monk Gak Jeong of Shaolin. And behind them -- five Plum Blossom Sword Masters, four extraordinary warrior monks, ten familiar Zhongnan masters, Hyeon Won-chang with his hero's headband dark with sweat. They had brought their martial siblings. Nine Sects connections who must have argued and pleaded and staked their reputations to get permission from their sect elders.

They had come. Not by order. Not by duty.

*Friendship.*

The Blood Flame Sect Leader had spoken of poor harvests and famine, of the disappearance of heroes. Could he say the same things while knowing these people?

Yeon-shin exchanged nods with each of them. Eye contact was all the greeting the situation allowed. There was no time for more. The Blood Flame Sect's swordmasters had let them through deliberately -- opened a corridor into the killing ground, intending to surround and slaughter. He could see the calculation in the Sixth Apostle's formation: draw the fastest fighters in, then close the trap.

Tap.

Cheong Myeong landed beside him, blue robe swirling, lightness technique so fast it was almost contemptuous. His spinning landing kicked up dust that caught the firelight. He patted Yeon-shin's shoulder and his one good eye swept the enemy formation with cool appraisal.

"These ones are quite strong," the Elf said. As if commenting on the weather.

Baek Mi-ryeo and Wei Ji Myo-hwa arrived next, raising counter winds that snapped banners from their poles. Then Won-chang, the killing-sword energy trailing from his drawn blade like black smoke. The confrontation settled into its shape: Yeon-shin and his allies in the center, the Blood Flame Sect's high-ranked members tightening around them like a fist.

"We've secured Lightning Genius' safety," Baek Mi-ryeo said. Her gaze ran over the Pure Blood Robe he still wore, checking for wounds beneath the crimson silk. "Getting out is the problem. We don't know how many exchanges the Sect Lord will need to kill the Blood Flame Sect Leader."

The Sixth Apostle stood at the head of the enemy formation. Gaunt. Hollow-cheeked. Smiling. The energy wrapping his body was the most powerful among the enemies -- a thrumming, dark weight that made the air around him feel heavier. The more skilled the warrior, the more sensitive their energy sense, and every allied master had been watching this man since the moment they arrived.

"Mount Hua, Zhongnan, Shaolin," the Apostle said, savoring the names like a man tasting wine. "The Nine Sects came too. Were you afraid of future troubles? The forces are pathetic. Without a single elder."

No one answered him. The politics of sect wars hung over the silence. Elder-level supreme masters stepping forth was a matter of the highest level -- equivalent to declaring war. Even orthodox sects who proclaimed chivalry put the reality of living people first. Fighting to extinction like Zhongnan and the Tyrant Sword Sect? Anyone would avoid it.

Meanwhile the Blood swordmasters stepped their footwork. Battle formations arranged themselves. The pressure of exploding momentum was tremendous.

"That guy -- isn't that an apostle?" Won-chang muttered belatedly, pointing at the Sixth.

The elites who had arrived first had already known. They had been watching.

Yeon-shin raised internal energy. He filled true qi into the Person Meeting point on both sides of his throat, the Sky Screen point above his vocal cords. Careful. Precise. He paid special attention to the operation.

*-We can kill every swordmaster here.*

Sound transmission to Baek Mi-ryeo alone.

*-Will you stand as dharma protector?*

A beat. Her puzzled expression shifted to understanding, then to something harder.

*-You are a blue rank who performed a long-term mission. You deserve respect.*

The enemies charged.

CLANG. BOOM.

Steel rang against steel. Energy waves crashed like drums against skin, and the sensation of it was amazing -- masters' clash, the resonance that could be felt in the bones. The Nine Sects masters guarded all directions while the Radiant Demon Wing companions formed a wall around Yeon-shin. The masters who had faced off became chaotically entangled. Blood sprayed. Someone screamed. A Plum Blossom Sword Master parried a crimson blade and counter-thrust, and the sound of his sword clearing flesh was sharp and final.

Yeon-shin closed his eyes.

He felt Cheong Myeong's presence at close range -- cold, precise, lethal. Baek Mi-ryeo's -- ink-dark and fluid. Won-chang's killing sword energy, steady as a hearthfire. They were protecting a warrior about to perform a complex true qi operation.

*Dharma protection.*

Their trust approached as untimely warmth.

WOONG.

The Demon-Destroying Azure Unyielding Palm blazed to life in his right hand. The arm dyed blue was different from before. Strange waves spread outward. Not sound this time -- not the sword song that ate his remaining days. *Dharma power.* He loaded dharma power instead of sound onto the radiating true qi, and the technique he had spent thirty sunrises planting like seeds throughout the headquarters answered. Hidden in railings and steps and rafters, dormant until this moment.

*This fight. I won't drag it out long.*

The activation signal pulsed outward in concentric waves. The scale of battle was large? His efforts focusing only on preparation for over thirty days were the same.

The Sixth Apostle sensed it first. His hollow face twisted. His body-protecting movement speed was like a ray of light as he launched himself toward Yeon-shin --

BOOM.

Marble fragments scattered in all directions. Gak Jeong and his four warrior monks intercepted, standing as one. The Eighteen Arhat Formation materialized in golden qi -- for an instant, the shape of a Buddha flickered above them, righteous energy waves spreading as one. The materialization of true qi. A battle formation multiplying power through joint attacks.

"The Buddha's will is upon your crowns!" Gak Jeong roared.

His crossed fists met the Apostle's strike. Shaolin Dragon Fist. Magnificent power sprouted from the impact -- and still he was driven back, his sandals carving furrows in marble. But he held. The four warrior monks roared with him, adding energy waves with the same technique, feeding their qi into the formation. They said all martial arts under heaven originated from Shaolin. Their underlying strength was truly enormous.

The Apostle was checked.

Yeon-shin was very grateful. He climbed.

Step by step, ascending the thirty-six stairs of affliction. Alone and peaceful within the escort of his Radiant Demon Wing companions. The dharma power in his hand intensified with each step, and the planted charges answered -- railings humming, steps vibrating, rafters singing.

The swordmasters who tried to approach faltered. The waves of the Demon-Destroying Azure Unyielding Palm grew increasingly dense.

WOOONG.

With each step, the dharma power grew stronger. Long preparation bloomed the flower of demon destruction. Righteous energy was being triggered in sequence from all places in the main hall.

Two Blood swordmasters' breathing faltered on the steps. A moment's gap. When the Mount Hua Plum Blossom Sword flashed, a head flew. The Plum Blossom Sword Master who struck the blow stared at his own sword, bewildered by the ease of it.

"What is this...?"

It happened everywhere. The swordmasters' defeats increased rapidly. The source was clear.

At the summit Yeon-shin opened his eyes. This was the place the Blood Flame Sect Leader had looked down upon all living things. He raised his foot to stamp a true step.

*It's hard.*

His upper dantian was burning white-hot inside his skull, the recoil of the sword song's resonance crushing inward. It felt as though his head was being pressed between two millstones. The inside of his mind was turning white.

He did not show it. The Shaolin monks who had met the Sixth Apostle's offensive were broken and bleeding. He alone possessed power specialized in opposing demonic practitioners now.

THUD.

He stamped his foot. That was the starting point.

Pale blue light rippled outward in eight directions. The Two Flame Blood Spirit Formation's key points transformed -- inverted, corrupted, remade into a demon-subduing formation of his own design. Even the fragments of sorcery carved by the Desolate Sect Lord completely changed. Every charge he had planted over thirty days detonated in sequence, starting from the railings and reaching the steps and rafters, explosive chains of impacts triggering other detonations.

All sorts of screams rang out across the Blood Flame Sect main hall that had become a battlefield.

They were the wails of blood demons, raw and naked with agony. The pain each felt nakedly filled the sky. Men and women writhed as though caught in the grip of Acala, the Immovable Wisdom King. Those who took direct hits from the overlapping dharma waves in the Sect Leader's hall died cleanly. Those who staggered were cut to pieces by orthodox blades, and the end was miserable compared to the momentum they had shown at the battle's start.

Demon-Destroying Devil-Eliminating. Fierce words. The orthodox martial artists' movements dealing with demonic practitioners were merciless.

But the Blood Flame Sect members in the courtyard did not look at their killers. Even while dying, they kept their eyes on the young man standing atop the steps.

His jet-black hair reaching his shoulders swayed in the waves. His spirit, light like a sun-touched sea, flashed. His extended hand was wrapped in deep blue radiance, the auspiciously undulating light creating a strange sight intermingling with the blood-colored Pure Blood Robe's hem.

"That bastard needs to die --"

"We brought in a death god."

"How can a lowborn's bearing be so noble..."

"If he had truly become our Grand Teacher."

The orthodox sect members' behavior was similar. While cutting off enemies' throats, they looked at Desolate Sect's Lightning Genius. The original plan had been different -- to endure, to trust in the Sect Lord's absolute power, to accept great sacrifices. But it was unnecessary. The young genius who had been held prisoner reversed the battle situation.

Before they knew it, only the Sixth Apostle remained standing among enemies.

Blood dripped from his fists. Several Nine Sects masters lay broken around him. He looked at Yeon-shin and momentary bewilderment cleared from his face, replaced by competitive spirit. Something that put aside fame and doctrine.

"Has the Great Illuminator descended," he breathed. An appearance unlike a Blood Flame Sect member.

Then, louder, admiration on his lips: "Your head, at least --"

He burst apart.

No warning. No flash. One moment standing, the next a spray of red mist and bone fragments scattered by gale-force wind that arrived after the killing blow. Along with his speaking mouth, his entire body shattered to pieces. Belatedly, tremendous winds swept away even the fragments of his corpse.

Step.

A woman alighted beside Yeon-shin, soft as a flower petal falling from above. Green eyes. Pale green hair, perfectly still. A presence that stiffened souls. Some grass seemed to tickle even his energy sense. There was something dreamlike sweeping his entire body.

"Sect Lord!"

Cheong Myeong and Baek Mi-ryeo dropped to their knees, blood-covered. Won-chang followed. Every Desolate Sect warrior in the courtyard prostrated. Many martial artists followed, kneeling or clasping hands. Supreme reverence or terror reflected on their various faces.

"Sect... Lord."

Yeon-shin barely opened his mouth. His entire body suddenly became heavy. The burden he had barely been enduring poured out like a flood breaking through a dam. He moved his head somewhat painfully.

Their eyes met.

Deep green pupils rippled in the sunlight. Her brilliant gaze looking at him intently. It was always like this when facing the Sect Lord -- the peerless master showing transcendence.

She slowly moved her lips.

"Your position will change."

Her voice seemed to ring from far away. He did not know when his eyelids closed. Before he knew it, his vision darkened like a dream.

"I first grant you a long sleep. Rest."

Rustle. Fabric touched his cheek. His body that was losing balance and tilting stopped.

*She caught me.*

Was he embraced? The Sect Lord spoke of long sleep. He might open his eyes in Desolate Sect. He truly did not want an escort or such.

*I wish you'd given me the fruit instead.*

Even just one piece.

Even his desperate idle thoughts slowly fluttered away. He fell into an abyss pressing down on him like a swamp. He laid down his consciousness without resistance.

Even the blade of his heart tempered through arduous murim journey was blunted.

*Just for a little while,* Yeon-shin thought.

It would be just for a very short while.

---
